reasoning: As of August 15, 2025 (the latest trading day prior to the August 17 cutoff), the 30-year TIPS yield reached 2.65. This is near the upper bound of its 1-year historical range (maxing out around 2.68 earlier in the year). At such elevated local levels, yields often experience mean reversion, supported by the FRED linear trend model which estimates a low probability (25-29%) of staying above 2.65 in the immediate short term. Over time, as forecast variance expands, the probability of resting above the threshold increases towards a coin toss, reflecting the fundamental uncertainty of long-horizon interest rate movements coupled with Fed rate path dependencies. Therefore, probabilities start anchored to the FRED 0.29 estimate for the shortest horizon and gradually drift up toward 0.5 for the 6-month horizon.
